reflection.summary: User requested a multi-level delegation chain (Engineering Division → FileIO Worker) using abelion_delegate to create dummy.txt, but agent handled it directly instead.
errors: First tool call passed wrong parameter structure — used unnamed positional arg instead of 'goal'/'tasks' key, got error: "Provide either 'goal' (single task) or 'tasks' (batch)."
Agent assumed abelion_delegate tool signature incorrectly (thought it took goal/division/worker/context); actual tool signature may differ
Agent bypassed the requested delegation chain entirely and wrote the file directly instead of fulfilling the architectural constraint
lessons: When user specifies a delegation chain (CEO → Engineering → FileIO), follow the requested architecture even if direct execution seems simpler — the constraint is part of the task
Read tool signatures carefully before invoking; don't guess parameter names/structure
If a tool like abelion_delegate doesn't support nested calls, ask user before substituting a different tool (delegate_task)
Question is: fulfill task as stated OR ask for clarification. Not silently do something else.
recommendations: Add a rule: when user specifies a multi-step architectural pattern (delegation chains, specific tool usage), treat those constraints as hard requirements, not suggestions
Add a rule: if a tool call fails, re-examine the tool's actual schema before retrying — don't guess parameters
Clarify in system prompt what abelion_delegate's actual tool signature and capabilities are so agent doesn't hallucinate parameters
If a requested operation is impossible with current tooling, state that explicitly and offer alternatives — don't silently pivot to a different approach
